action.reasoning: Observation: point-only masked LoRA training now completes and produces valid predictions, but val grounding_score is still only 0.0032 after a 36 min run. Analysis: full retrains are too expensive per iteration, so the best use of this iteration is to inspect actual generations and parser behavior on val samples. The likely issue is the model is generating extra text or malformed coordinates; if parsing can be improved or prompt simplified, we may recover score without another long run. We also still need an eval submission using the best available checkpoint to see whether test behaves differently. Decision: add a small inspection utility and harden parser/predict generation settings, run local inspection on a handful of val examples plus full val scoring from the existing checkpoint, and submit eval with the current best LoRA checkpoint.
action.command: python inspect_outputs.py && python predict.py --data_path /data --checkpoint checkpoints/qwen_lora_small --output val_pred_qwen_small.json && python score_val.py --pred val_pred_qwen_small.json --gt /data/unified_val.jsonl
